Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-13 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's data shows ELA dominating the momentum rankings with a score of 96.0 and rank #1, driven by exceptional returns across all timeframes—particularly a 274.62% one-year return and a 91.58% three-month surge. What stands out is ELA's extreme ratio consistency (4 out of 4 ratio passes) and its ability to outperform SPY and QQQ significantly (up 247.29% and 233.88% versus those benchmarks over one year), while maintaining a near-perfect bad_spy_score of 3.78, indicating strong resilience even on down market days. In contrast, PUMP (rank #2) shows solid momentum with a 175.83% one-year return but trails ELA substantially on both absolute performance and relative outperformance metrics, posting only 148.51% versus SPY's one-year return. Both tickers pass all technical gates (above 30/50/100/200-day SMAs) and show "confirmed" volatility flags, suggesting genuine momentum rather than noise. ELA's 36.11x ratio from 1-week to 1-day returns is notably extreme, warranting attention to whether this reflects sustainable acceleration or potential consolidation ahead.
